title: Repository, Locks, Metadata, and Provenance Scaffold
objective: Create an independent, portable, locked, testable, and evidence-producing project foundation without implementing product workflows.
scope: Version, citation, license, and CodeMeta metadata
PromptEvent, Ticket, HumanDecision, Run, AssetRights, and ReleaseManifest schemas
CI, security scanning, SBOM, tests, and P001 evidence

acceptance.criterion: A documented single command restores the locked Python and R environments from a clean checkout.
Format, lint, type, unit, metadata, and schema checks pass with zero errors.
Two frozen Python syncs preserve the lock and direct dependency versions; the R lock reports a consistent state.
PromptEvent capture modes and hashes validate without representing summary-only content as native.
HumanDecision records distinguish human ownership from AI assistance and validate against the schema.
Repository scans find no secret, user corpus, or local absolute path in versionable files.
Container base references are digest-pinned; local build verification is reported truthfully.

changed_files: P000 canonical contracts, ADR-0001 through ADR-0008, and agent handoff documents imported as the initial repository baseline
ADR-0009, README, VERSION, LICENSE, CITATION.cff, codemeta.json, and repository policy files
pyproject.toml, uv.lock, renv.lock, Python package scaffold, and R dependency scaffold
PromptEvent, Ticket, HumanDecision, Run, AssetRights, and ReleaseManifest schemas and records
Bootstrap, verification, metadata, repository scan, SBOM, security, test, and CI files
P001 evidence report, environment summary, clean-clone verification, and two Run records

commands.result: Created the independent Delta repository on branch main; the parent repository ignores the complete directory.
Initial lock attempt correctly rejected incompatible legacy license metadata; metadata was corrected before retry.
Created the frozen Python dependency lock.
Restored the locked Python and R project environments and reported a synchronized R lock.
Passed format, lint, strict type, 24 test, metadata, schema, repository, supply-chain, and R lock checks with 100% measured Python source coverage.
Generated Python and R SBOMs, audited 44 runtime dependencies with zero reported known vulnerabilities, and produced zero secret findings after structural allowlisting.
A new clone of commit 26131a8 restored both environments, passed all gates, and ended with zero versionable changes.
The current macOS R/Tcl-Tk build requires XQuartz; this disclosed platform limitation does not represent a passed scientific execution test and remains assigned to P006.
